contents.value: **SELECT** — Redshift SQL keyword.
**FROM** — Redshift SQL keyword.
**WHERE** — Redshift SQL keyword.
**JOIN** — Redshift SQL keyword.
**LEFT** — Redshift SQL keyword.
**COUNT** — Built-in function/command in Redshift SQL.
**SUM** — Built-in function/command in Redshift SQL.
**AVG** — Built-in function/command in Redshift SQL.
**MIN** — Built-in function/command in Redshift SQL.
**MAX** — Built-in function/command in Redshift SQL.

```sql
INSERT INTO table (col1, col2) VALUES (val1, val2)
```
**INSERT** adds new rows to a table.

```sql
UPDATE table SET col = val WHERE condition
```
**UPDATE** modifies existing rows.

```sql
DELETE FROM table WHERE condition
```
**DELETE** removes rows from a table.

An **index** speeds up queries by creating an optimized lookup structure.
```sql
CREATE INDEX idx ON table(column);
```

A **view** is a virtual table defined by a query.
```sql
CREATE VIEW name AS SELECT ...;
```

A **transaction** groups operations that must succeed or fail as a unit.
```sql
BEGIN; ... COMMIT; -- or ROLLBACK;
```

**Aggregate functions** compute a single result from a set of rows.
```sql
COUNT(*), SUM(col), AVG(col), MIN(col), MAX(col)
```

A **subquery** is a query nested inside another query.
```sql
SELECT * FROM t WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM t2)
```

A **Common Table Expression** defines a named temporary result set.
```sql
WITH cte AS (SELECT ...) SELECT * FROM cte
```

**Constraints** enforce rules on data in tables.
```sql
PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE, NOT NULL, CHECK
```
**NULL** represents the absence of a value. Use IS NULL / IS NOT NULL to test.

**Window functions** compute values across a set of rows related to the current row.
```sql
ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY col ORDER BY col)
```

**GROUP BY** groups rows with the same values for aggregate calculations.
```sql
SELECT dept, COUNT(*) FROM emp GROUP BY dept
```

```redshift
COPY table_name FROM 's3://bucket/path' IAM_ROLE 'arn:aws:iam::role' [FORMAT AS CSV|JSON|AVRO|PARQUET] [DELIMITER 'char'] [IGNOREHEADER n] [GZIP|LZOP|BZIP2]
```
Loads data from Amazon S3, DynamoDB, or remote hosts into a Redshift table using massively parallel processing for high-throughput bulk ingestion of large datasets.

```redshift
UNLOAD ('select_statement') TO 's3://bucket/path' IAM_ROLE 'arn:aws:iam::role' [FORMAT AS CSV|JSON|PARQUET] [PARALLEL ON|OFF] [MAXFILESIZE n MB]
```
Exports the results of a query to one or more files on Amazon S3, supporting parallel writes across slices for fast extraction of large result sets.

```redshift
GETDATE() -> TIMESTAMP
```
Returns the current date and time in UTC as a timestamp value, equivalent to CURRENT_TIMESTAMP but specific to Redshift SQL dialect for compatibility purposes.

```redshift
DATEADD(datepart, interval, expression) -> TIMESTAMP|DATE
```
Adds a specified number of intervals (years, months, days, hours, minutes, seconds) to a date or timestamp expression and returns the resulting value.

```redshift
DATEDIFF(datepart, start_date, end_date) -> BIGINT
```
Calculates the difference between two date or timestamp expressions in the specified date part units such as years, months, days, hours, minutes, or seconds.

```redshift
NVL(expression1, expression2) -> value
```
Returns the first non-null expression among its arguments, functioning as a null-coalescing operator equivalent to COALESCE with exactly two parameters in standard SQL.

```redshift
LISTAGG(expression [, delimiter]) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY sort_expression) -> VARCHAR
```
Aggregates values from multiple rows into a single concatenated string with an optional delimiter, ordered by the specified sort expression within each group.

```redshift
DISTKEY (column_name)
```
Table distribution style attribute that specifies the column used to distribute rows across compute node slices, optimizing join performance by co-locating matching rows on the same node.

```redshift
SORTKEY (column_name [, ...]) | COMPOUND SORTKEY (...) | INTERLEAVED SORTKEY (...)
```
Defines the sort order of data stored on disk within each slice, enabling zone map filtering to skip blocks during query execution and dramatically reducing I/O for filtered queries.

```redshift
DISTSTYLE EVEN | KEY | ALL | AUTO
```
Specifies how table rows are distributed across compute node slices; EVEN distributes round-robin, KEY uses a column hash, ALL replicates to every node, and AUTO lets Redshift choose.

```redshift
APPROXIMATE COUNT(DISTINCT column) -> BIGINT
```
Returns an approximate distinct count using a HyperLogLog algorithm, providing significantly faster results than exact COUNT DISTINCT for large datasets with minimal error margin.

```redshift
CONVERT_TIMEZONE('source_timezone', 'target_timezone', timestamp) -> TIMESTAMP
```
Converts a timestamp from one time zone to another, supporting IANA time zone names and UTC offsets for accurate cross-timezone date and time calculations in analytics.

```redshift
SET WLM_QUERY_SLOT_COUNT TO n
```
Temporarily increases the number of workload management query slots allocated to the current session, allowing a single query to use more memory for complex operations.

```redshift
SELECT * FROM STL_QUERY WHERE ...
```
System table that logs information about database queries including query text, start and end times, and status, essential for monitoring and troubleshooting query performance.

```redshift
CREATE EXTERNAL SCHEMA schema_name FROM DATA CATALOG DATABASE 'db' IAM_ROLE 'arn' CREATE EXTERNAL DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS
```
Redshift Spectrum enables querying data stored in Amazon S3 using external tables without loading, leveraging the Glue Data Catalog for schema definitions and partition management.

```redshift
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```redshift
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```redshift
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```redshift
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```redshift
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```redshift
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```redshift
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.
